specification:
Type: infographic

    Purpose: Help students select the most appropriate budgeting method based on their personality, goals, and situation (Bloom's: Evaluating, Creating)

    Layout: Three-column comparison with interactive assessment tool

    Top section: "Three Popular Budgeting Approaches"

    Column 1: 50/30/20 Rule
    Icon: Pie chart divided into three sections
    Tagline: "Simple percentages, big impact"

    Complexity: ⭐ (1/5 stars)
    Time required: 1-2 hours/month
    Detail level: Low
    Flexibility: High

    Best for:
    - Budgeting beginners
    - People who dislike tracking details
    - Variable income earners
    - Those who want simple guidelines

    How it works:
    - 50% of income to needs
    - 30% to wants
    - 20% to savings/debt
    - Track by category percentage, not every transaction

    Column 2: Zero-Based Budgeting
    Icon: Spreadsheet with rows summing to zero
    Tagline: "Every dollar has a job"

    Complexity: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5 stars)
    Time required: 3-5 hours/month
    Detail level: Very high
    Flexibility: Medium

    Best for:
    - Detail-oriented people
    - Those with specific financial goals
    - People wanting maximum control
    - Aggressive debt payers

    How it works:
    - Allocate 100% of income before month begins
    - Every dollar assigned to a category
    - Adjust throughout month as needed
    - Income minus expenses = $0

    Column 3: Envelope Method
    Icon: Envelope with cash
    Tagline: "Cash makes it real"

    Complexity: ⭐⭐ (2/5 stars)
    Time required: 2-3 hours/month
    Detail level: Medium
    Flexibility: Low

    Best for:
    - Visual/tactile learners
    - Card overspenders
    - People breaking bad habits
    - Those wanting physical limits

    How it works:
    - Withdraw cash at start of month
    - Divide into category envelopes
    - Spend only envelope cash
    - Empty envelope = stop spending

    Middle section: Interactive Assessment

    "Answer 5 questions to find your best method"

    Q1: How much detail do you enjoy?
    - [ ] Minimal - I want big picture only → +1 for 50/30/20
    - [ ] Some - I like categories but not every transaction → +1 for Envelope
    - [ ] Maximum - I want to track everything → +1 for Zero-based

    Q2: What's your main budgeting challenge?
    - [ ] I don't save enough → +1 for 50/30/20 or Zero-based
    - [ ] I overspend with credit/debit cards → +1 for Envelope
    - [ ] I don't know where my money goes → +1 for Zero-based
    - [ ] I need something simple to start → +1 for 50/30/20

    Q3: How much time can you spend on budgeting?
    - [ ] 1-2 hours/month maximum → +1 for 50/30/20
    - [ ] 2-4 hours/month is fine → +1 for Envelope
    - [ ] 4+ hours/month, no problem → +1 for Zero-based

    Q4: Do you have variable or steady income?
    - [ ] Very variable (freelance, commission, hourly) → +1 for 50/30/20
    - [ ] Somewhat variable → +1 for Envelope
    - [ ] Very steady (salary, regular paycheck) → +1 for Zero-based

    Q5: What's your personality?
    - [ ] Flexible, big-picture thinker → +1 for 50/30/20
    - [ ] Need physical/tangible systems → +1 for Envelope
    - [ ] Detail-oriented, love optimization → +1 for Zero-based

    Results:
    "Your recommended method: [Highest score]"
    "But remember: Try different methods to find what works for YOU. Many people combine approaches!"

    Bottom section: Hybrid Approaches

    "Mix and Match"
    - Use 50/30/20 overall framework
    - Apply zero-based budgeting to the 50% needs category
    - Use envelope method for the 30% wants category
    - This combines simplicity, control, and spending awareness

    Visual style:
    - Three distinct branded colors (blue, green, orange)
    - Icons and illustrations for each method
    - Clean comparison table layout
    - Interactive quiz with immediate feedback

    Implementation: HTML/CSS/JavaScript with quiz scoring logic
